statement: The dominant source of duplicate apply/finalize churn is missing per-job lease/claim ownership, allowing concurrent running-job workflows on the same job.
rationale: Scheduler can dispatch running jobs repeatedly and job workflows do not claim ownership before polling/finalizing, matching observed repeated job_finalized and duplicate apply patterns.
keyAssumptions: Multiple running-job actions overlap in time for the same job.
Current data races are not fully mitigated by status checks alone.
validationCriteria: After introducing job claim lease, jobs_finalized_multiple_times drops to 0 on matched window run.
After introducing job claim lease, duplicate_apply_success_total drops by >80%.
critiquePoints: Even with job claims, repeated no-op patch writes may persist if duplicate branch still patches/emits.
Batch mode may still duplicate under finalizing lease expiry.
questions: Should job claim lease be separate for queued and running handlers?
How should lease heartbeat be handled during long provider latency?
